meaning: n.最低票价的舱位,士官的二等室,操纵,驾驶,掌舵
examples: Passengers traveling in the steerage paid the lowest fares.
He was re-elected for his miraculous steerage of the country through an economic disaster.
The first-class cabins are too expensive, second class is stifling and third is in steerage.
At one point, the smell of laundry drifted into the dining room, a whiff of steerage.
Doubtless he had decided that it was outrageous for me to fly steerage, sound chap that he was.
